channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Pathogenesis & Basic Science
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Pathogenesis & Basic Science

item.title: IL-10-STAT3 axis preserves epithelial mitochondrial homeostasis during bacterial challenge.
item.description: Gut microbes - Increasing evidence implicates mitochondrial dysfunction in the pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), with the IBD-associated pathobiont adherent-invasive E. coli-LF82 (AIEC) promoting ...

item.title: Phytochemicals in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Targeting Oxidative Stress for Improved Gut Health.
item.description: Phytotherapy research : PTR - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a group of chronic gastrointestinal inflammatory disorders, primarily including ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD). Reactive oxygen species (ROS) p...

item.title: Oral administration of polyelectrolyte modified curcumin-loaded egg protein nanoparticles for suppression of inflammation in colitis mice.
item.description: International journal of biological macromolecules - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory disease affecting the large intestine, resulting in inflammation to the mucosa and submucosa of the colon. Overproduction of reactive oxygen specie...

item.title: Targeting the TLR4/PAD4-mediated formation of macrophage extracellular traps by curcumin attenuates plaque destabilization in ulcerative colitis-associated atherosclerosis.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - Acute ulcerative colitis elevates the risk of plaque rupture in patients with atherosclerosis, potentially triggering acute cardiovascular events. Clinical treatment strategies capable of simultane...
